 16 │     --skip-init-end-tasks                # If set, init and end tasks are skipped

nushell/nushell#10242.body:
### Describe the bug

Errors in `export-env` are sometimes not reported (when running `nu` or `overlay use`, but there might be other cases). 

### How to reproduce

```nushell
export-env { let-env FOO = 'bar' }
```

Save the above into `spam.nu` and call `overlay use spam.nu` and `nu spam.nu`.  You'll see that in both cases nothing happens.

### Expected behavior

```
Error: nu::shell::removed_command

  × Removed command: let-env
   ╭─[/home/kubouch/git/forks/nupm/spam.nu:1:1]
 1 │ export-env { let-env FOO = 'bar' }
   ·              ───┬───
   ·                 ╰── 'let-env' has been removed from Nushell. Please use '$env.<environment variable> = ...' instead.
   ╰────
```


### Screenshots

_No response_

### Configuration

| key                | value                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| version            | 0.84.1                                                                                                                     |
| branch             | main                                                                                                                       |
| commit_hash        | 54394fe9af746230c6c7cbb399c54672477bfa84                                                                                   |
| build_os           | linux-x86_64                                                                                                               |
| build_target       | x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                                                   |
| rust_version       | rustc 1.70.0 (90c541806 2023-05-31)                                                                                        |
| rust_channel       | 1.70.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                                            |
| cargo_version      | cargo 1.70.0 (ec8a8a0ca 2023-04-25)                                                                                        |
| build_time         | 2023-09-05 17:43:59 +03:00                                                                                                 |
| build_rust_channel | release                                                                                                                    |
| allocator          | standard                                                                                                                   |
| features           | default, extra, sqlite, trash, which, zip                                                                                  |
| installed_plugins  | from eml, from ics, from ini, from vcf, gstat, highlight, hist, inc, plot, query, query json, query web, query xml, xyplot |

### Additional context

Calling `use spam.nu` reports the error correctly.
nushell/nushell#10242.title: `overlay use` and `nu` do not report error inside `export-env`

nushell/nushell#13275.body:
### Describe the bug

Removing a symlink of a directory, if ending with `/`, removes not the symlink, but the existing directory.

This is especially troublesome since autocompletion automatically appends the trailing `/`.

### How to reproduce

```
> mkdir foo
> ln -s foo foo-link
> rm foo-<tab>  # autocompletes to "foo-link"
> ls -l
# lists only foo-link
```


### Expected behavior

Two options (not mutually exclusive):

* Both `rm foo-link` and `rm foo-link/` should remove the symlink, not the original path. Such a subtle difference as a trailing slash should not have this severe impact on semantics.
* Autocompletion shouldn't append `/` to symlinks pointing at directories.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Configuration

| key                | value                                                                                                  |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| version            | 0.95.1                                                                                                 |
| major              | 0                                                                                                      |
| minor              | 95                                                                                                     |
| patch              | 1                                                                                                      |
| branch             | main                                                                                                   |
| commit_hash        | 153b45bc63b6b65a34e3efa966a2fb4c8af999ab                                                               |
| build_os           | linux-x86_64                                                                                           |
| build_target       | x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                               |
| rust_version       | rustc 1.77.2 (25ef9e3d8 2024-04-09)                                                                    |
| rust_channel       | 1.77.2-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                        |
| cargo_version      | cargo 1.77.2 (e52e36006 2024-03-26)                                                                    |
| build_time         | 2024-06-29 10:28:36 +03:00                                                                             |
| build_rust_channel | release                                                                                                |
| allocator          | mimalloc                                                                                               |
| features           | default, sqlite, system-clipboard, trash                                                               |
| installed_plugins  | explore, formats 0.95.1, gstat 0.95.1, highlight 1.2.0+0.95.0, inc 0.95.1, polars 0.95.1, query 0.95.1 |

### Additional context

_No response_
nushell/nushell#13275.title: Removing symlink ending with `/` removes the original path

nushell/nushell#13720.body:
### Related problem

A demonstration that `par-each` doesn't stream:

```nushell
[7,8,9,10] | each {|x| sleep 1sec; $x} | par-each {|el| $el} | each {|x| print $x}
```

### Describe the solution you'd like

The above snippet would emit values immediately after they are received by `par-each`

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context and details

This wouldn't be possible when using the `--keep-order` flag
nushell/nushell#13720.title: add streaming support to par-each

nushell/nushell#14504.body:
### Describe the bug

I have a custom command for running git with an env, as follows:

```nu
def --wrapped git [...x] { TZ=UTC ^git ...$x }
```

When working with this new `git`, nushell does not invoke shell completions for positional arguments like file names. It does seem to work correctly for flags.

### How to reproduce

Configure an external completer that logs the spans it gets:

```nu
let external_completer = {|spans|
    $"SPANS: ($spans)\n" | save --append ~/nu-log
    # ...
    # I am using the fish completer but it shouldn't matter here
}

$env.config = {
    # ...

    completions: {
        case_sensitive: false
        quick: true
        partial: true
        algorithm: "prefix"
        external: {
            enable: true
            max_results: 100
            completer: $external_completer
        }
        use_ls_colors: true
    }
    # ...
```

Get a baseline, this behavior is expected:

```
$ git commit --**TAB** # shows completions like --all, --allow-empty
$ cat ~/nu-log
SPANS: [git, commit, --]
$ git commit --fixup **TAB** # space after --fixup, shows completions from the previous commits
$ cat ~/nu-log
SPANS: [git, commit, --]
SPANS: [git, commit, --fixup, ]
```

Add a wrapper definition and reload the shell:

```nu
# Always commit in UTC
def --wrapped git [...x] { TZ=UTC ^git ...$x }
```

Delete no-log and reload the shell, retry the above

```
$ git commit --**TAB** # works correctly
$ cat ~/nu-log
SPANS: [git, commit, --]
$ git commit --fixup **TAB** # space after --fixup, shows no completions
$ cat ~/nu-log
SPANS: [git, commit, --]
```

The flag completions work fine, but trying to complete an argument after `--fixup` doesn't seem to invoke the completion script. Results seem similar for anything positional like `git add **TAB**` which should list file.

### Expected behavior

Completion should work the same with and without the `--wrapped` function.

### Configuration

```
| key                | value                                          |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------- |
| version            | 0.100.0                                        |
| major              | 0                                              |
| minor              | 100                                            |
| patch              | 0                                              |
| branch             |                                                |
| commit_hash        |                                                |
| build_os           | macos-aarch64                                  |
| build_target       | aarch64-apple-darwin                           |
| rust_version       | rustc 1.82.0 (f6e511eec 2024-10-15) (Homebrew) |
| cargo_version      | cargo 1.82.0                                   |
| build_time         | 2024-11-13 03:28:47 +00:00                     |
| build_rust_channel | release                                        |
| allocator          | mimalloc                                       |
| features           | default, sqlite, trash                         |
| installed_plugins  |                                                |
```
nushell/nushell#14504.title: `def --wrapped` does not invoke shell completions for positional arguments

nushell/nushell#14946.body:
### Describe the bug

While trying to implement automatic palette changes based on whether the dark mode is currently enabled or not, I found that even though I can change `$env.config.color_config` from inside the hook, nushell ignores those changes (actual colours stay the same), even though it shows the changes to `$env.config.color_config`.

However, if the same `color_config` property is touched (even though there's no real change) from the REPL, the change gets applied (marked with `!!!!` on the following screenshot).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6187582b-3f89-4cdd-bb42-2a25f0dd046a)

### How to reproduce

1. Add a `pre_prompt` (or `pre_execution`) hook:
    ```
    $env.config.hooks.pre_prompt = [{ $env.config.color_config.row_index = "cyan" }]
    ```
2. Run `ls`

### Expected behavior

Row indexes should be coloured cyan, but they are not changed until `$env.config.color_config` is touched from the REPL.

### Configuration

| key                | value                                                                                              |
| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| version            | 0.101.0                                                                                            |
| major              | 0                                                                                                  |
| minor              | 101                                                                                                |
| patch              | 0                                                                                                  |
| branch             |                                                                                                    |
| commit_hash        | fb2610904997f54f50ad52095948bede81695f31                                                           |
| build_os           | linux-x86_64                                                                                       |
| build_target       | x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                           |
| rust_version       | rustc 1.81.0 (eeb90cda1 2024-09-04)                                                                |
| rust_channel       | 1.81.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                    |
| cargo_version      | cargo 1.81.0 (2dbb1af80 2024-08-20)                                                                |
| build_time         | 2024-12-22 15:47:14 +00:00                                                                         |
| build_rust_channel | release                                                                                            |
| allocator          | mimalloc                                                                                           |
| features           | default, sqlite, static-link-openssl, trash                                                        |
| installed_plugins  | formats 0.101.0, gstat 0.101.0, inc 0.101.0, plotters 0.1.2+0.100.0, polars 0.101.0, query 0.101.0 |
nushell/nushell#14946.title: Nushell ignores changes to `$env.config.color_config` made from hooks

nushell/nushell#15105.body:
Most of our commands have a set of search terms to find them when you press `F1` or use the `help -f` functionality.
We went through a lot of command and added useful terms serendipidously. 

https://github.com/nushell/nushell/blob/2e1b6acc0ea1ca796061163dfa322dac382cc9fd/crates/nu-command/src/filters/append.rs#L34-L36

But at the same time in our documentation already curates a quite extensive translation table going from several other shell tools or programming languages to Nushell.

- [ ] [Coming from bash](https://www.nushell.sh/book/coming_from_bash.html)
- [ ] [Coming from CMD](https://www.nushell.sh/book/coming_from_cmd.html)
- [ ] [Nu map from other shells and domain specific languages](https://www.nushell.sh/book/nushell_map.html)
  - [ ] Powershell with its cmdlet name may be a special interesting case to cover to make the transition for folks easier 
- [ ] [Nu Map from Imperative Languages](https://www.nushell.sh/book/nushell_map_imperative.html#nu-map-from-imperative-languages)
- [ ] [Nu Map from Functional Languages](https://www.nushell.sh/book/nushell_map_functional.html#nu-map-from-functional-languages)

It seems worth going through those tables and checking the search terms and adding any missing concepts (as long as they don't add further confusion, e.g. don't add `awk` to all commands where you would solve it with a call to `awk` when using bash)

(feel free to tackle just one of the tables)
nushell/nushell#15105.title: Ensure Nushell commands can be found by the names from other languages

nushell/nushell#15119.body:
### Describe the bug

When both a format (`-f`) and a timezone (`-l`) is set,
`into datetime` ignores the timezone.

### How to reproduce

`1667816159 | $in * 1_000_000_000 | into datetime  -z l`
vs
`1667816159 | into datetime -f "%s" -z l`

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9d28b15f-b9ab-47af-b23c-5016ac880054)

### Expected behavior

I expected nu to return the same result for both commands.

Or at least return an error. 
It should not just ignore an option silently.

### Configuration

`version | transpose key value | to md --pretty`
| key                | value                                                             |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| version            | 0.101.0                                                           |
| major              | 0                                                                 |
| minor              | 101                                                               |
| patch              | 0                                                                 |
| branch             |                                                                   |
| commit_hash        |                                                                   |
| build_os           | linux-x86_64                                                      |
| build_target       | x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                          |
| rust_version       | rustc 1.83.0 (90b35a623 2024-11-26) (built from a source tarball) |
| cargo_version      | cargo 1.83.0 (5ffbef321 2024-10-29)                               |
| build_time         | 1980-01-01 00:00:00 +00:00                                        |
| build_rust_channel | release                                                           |
| allocator          | mimalloc                                                          |
| features           | default, sqlite, trash                                            |
| installed_plugins  |                                                                   |

This is on nixos, in case the weird build_time confuses you.
nushell/nushell#15119.title: into datetime silently ignores timezone flag when format option is set

nushell/nushell#16011.body:
### Describe the bug

When converting round numbers to certain formats (notably csv, tsv, json and possibly others) and back, they are incorrectly cast as integers.
Strangely enough ('to nuon' and 'to ndjson') correctly preserves the '.0'.

Issue was brought up and solved for 'to nuon' already.
https://github.com/nushell/nushell/issues/5767#issue-1269577668
https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/5768#issue-1269711256

### How to reproduce

```
> [{x:1.0}] | to csv | from csv | get x | $in.0 | describe
int

> [{x:1.0}] | to tsv | from tsv | get x | $in.0 | describe
int

> [{x:1.0}] | to json | from json | get x | $in.0 | describe
int

> [{x:1.0}] | to nuon | from nuon | get x | $in.0 | describe
float

> [{x:1.0}] | to ndjson | from ndjson | get x | $in.0 | describe
float
```

### Expected behavior

Indentical behavior to how 'to nuon' and 'to ndjson' converts floats into text representation.
Consistent behavior across all output formats (ie. to csv, to tsv, to json ... and all the others).

### Configuration

| key                | value                                                                  |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| version            | 0.105.1                                                                |
| major              | 0                                                                      |
| minor              | 105                                                                    |
| patch              | 1                                                                      |
| branch             |                                                                        |
| commit_hash        | 1fe62ee6132372453d6bfeb95978ee61a2b77f3b                               |
| build_os           | windows-x86_64                                                         |
| build_target       | x86_64-pc-windows-msvc                                                 |
| rust_version       | rustc 1.85.1 (4eb161250 2025-03-15)                                    |
| rust_channel       | 1.85.1-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc                                          |
| cargo_version      | cargo 1.85.1 (d73d2caf9 2024-12-31)                                    |
| build_time         | 2025-06-10 19:56:11 +00:00                                             |
| build_rust_channel | release                                                                |
| allocator          | standard                                                               |
| features           | default, sqlite, trash                                                 |
| installed_plugins  | formats 0.102.0, len 0.1.0, plot 0.91.1, polars 0.102.0, query 0.103.0 |
nushell/nushell#16011.title: Conversion via 'to' does not preserve float datatypes correctly and is inconsistent across output formats

nushell/nushell#16085.body:
### Related problem

Given an `example.txt` file with the contents:
```txt
user=0 action="bar"
user=0 action=""
user=0
```

And parsing it with nushell:
```nushell
> open example.txt | parse --regex '(?m)^user=(?<user>[0-9]+)(?: action="(?<action>[^"]*)")?$'
╭───┬──────┬────────╮
│ # │ user │ action │
├───┼──────┼────────┤
│ 0 │ 0    │ bar    │
│ 1 │ 0    │        │
│ 2 │ 0    │        │
╰───┴──────┴────────╯
```

We can't tell item 1 and 2 apart. If unmatched groups were null we could check `$it.action != null` to see if the group matched at all.

Having `null` capture groups can also make code dealing with or-patterns more correct and understandable:
```nushell
if $it.number != null {
    # do this
} else if $it.string != null {
    # do that
} else {
    # ...
}
```

### Describe the solution you'd like

Make `parse` return `null` for unmatched capture groups.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context and details

If you agree I'd be happy to open a PR implementing this change.
nushell/nushell#16085.title: Make `parse` return `null` for unmatched capture groups

Add `(` at the end of the last argument, making it `12345(` 3. The command line will show `ls | where a b 1234512345(` ### Expected behavior No text duplication. ### Configuration | key | value | | ------------------ | ---------------------------------------- | | version | 0.105.1 | | major | 0 | | minor | 105 | | patch | 1 | | branch | | | commit_hash | 1fe62ee6132372453d6bfeb95978ee61a2b77f3b | | build_os | windows-x86_64 | | build_target | x86_64-pc-windows-msvc | | rust_version | rustc 1.85.1 (4eb161250 2025-03-15) | | rust_channel | 1.85.1-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc | | cargo_version | cargo 1.85.1 (d73d2caf9 2024-12-31) | | build_time | 2025-06-10 19:56:11 +00:00 | | build_rust_channel | release | | allocator | standard | | features | default, sqlite, trash | | installed_plugins | | Windows 11 using Windows terminal with default settings.

nushell/nushell#16186.body:
### Describe the bug

Parsing repeated open parens causes `nu` to consume many GBs of memory and crashing.

Found while trying to fuzz `nu-parser`'s `parse_with_keywords` target.

This behavior sounds similar to #15241 in conceptual terms (i.e. many unclosed nested AST structures) but is different in manifestation that it does not fail with a stack overflow but a general memory leak.

More closely related in behavior to #13944 

### How to reproduce

use `ulimit -m` before launching the `nu` under test, to limit the consumed memory.
Type `(((((((((((((((((((` into the repl (or a few more or less `(` depending on the available memory)
Syntax highlighting stalls and the process gets killed.

Saving the same sequence to a script `test.nu` and running `nu test.nu` does not trigger for the same number of open `(` and instead reports back with the error:
```
Error: nu::parser::unexpected_eof

  × Unexpected end of code.
   ╭─[/home/stefan/nushell/crates/nu-parser/fuzz/testrepr.nu:1:20]
 1 │ (((((((((((((((((((
   ·                    ┬
   ·                    ╰── expected closing )
   ╰────
```

### Expected behavior

No memory leak, no stack overflow, just appropriate parse errors

### Configuration

| key                | value                               |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------- |
| version            | 0.105.1                             |
| major              | 0                                   |
| minor              | 105                                 |
| patch              | 1                                   |
| branch             |                                     |
| commit_hash        |                                     |
| build_os           | linux-x86_64                        |
| build_target       | x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu            |
| rust_version       | rustc 1.88.0 (6b00bc388 2025-06-23) |
| rust_channel       | stable-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu     |
| cargo_version      | cargo 1.88.0 (873a06493 2025-05-10) |
| build_time         | 2025-07-06 20:25:09 +02:00          |
| build_rust_channel | release                             |
| allocator          | standard                            |
| features           | default, sqlite, trash              |
| installed_plugins  | plotters 0.1.7+0.105.1              |
nushell/nushell#16186.title: Parsing repeated `(` / opened subexpressions goes OOM

nushell/nushell#16200.body: ### Describe the bug Hi, I am not sure if this is really a bug, or should be labeled as a feature request. Feel free to change :) Using `input list` with ANSI colored strings results in incorrectly colored items. The reason for that is, that there is no ANSI reset sequence at the end of items. ### Why this matters? When building a list dynamically from user defined data, the user might include ANSI codes, which will mess up the formatting. Many people may not consider this when building scripts that load raw data. Tables / lists handle ANSI correctly (since the border color overwrites the color before): `input list` does not: When selecting a different item, everything gets colored: As a workaround, a manual ANSI reset sequence can be put at the end of the item: ### How to reproduce ```nu ["this" "is" "a" $"(ansi red)colored" "list"] | input list ``` ### Expected behavior I expected nu to correctly escape the item coloring. ### Configuration | key | value | | ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | | version | 0.105.1 | | major | 0 | | minor | 105 | | patch | 1 | | branch | | | commit_hash | | | build_os | linux-x86_64 | | build_target | x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu | | rust_version | rustc 1.88.0 (6b00bc388 2025-06-23) (built from a source tarball) | | cargo_version | cargo 1.88.0 (873a06493 2025-05-10) | | build_time | 1980-01-01 00:00:00 +00:00 | | build_rust_channel | release | | allocator | standard | | features | default, sqlite, trash | | installed_plugins | gstat 0.105.1, polars 0.105.1, query 0.105.1 |
nushell/nushell#16200.title: `input list` doesn't support ANSI colored strings well

nushell/nushell#16217.body:
### Describe the bug

`group-by --to-table` does not always return a table (a list)

### How to reproduce

> `[] | group-by --to-table something`

```
╭──────────────╮
│ empty record │
╰──────────────╯
````

### Expected behavior

I expect the result to be an empty _list_ instead.

nushell/nushell#16283.body:
### Related problem

A minor but recurring itch: a lot of the time when I'm using the spread operator to build lists or external command calls, I end up having to insert a lot of `... else {[]}` or `default []` boilerplate:

```
^some_external ...(if $foo != null {[--foo $foo]} else {[]}) ...($bar.baz.extra_args? | default [])
```

It doesn't seem to me that allowing the expression to be spliced to evaluate to `null` and treating it as an empty collection would result in footguns of the javascript quality. Also:

- some commands already treat a `null` input as "empty collection" (e.g. `length`, `is-empty`...).
- in string interpolation, something evaluating to `null` already behaves like the empty string

Paired with some counterpart of `default` defined like this:

```
def then [cls: closure] {
    if $in != null {
        do $cls $in
    }
}
```

this would allow the above example to be made much more concise:

```
^some_external ...($foo | then {[--foo $in]}) ...($bar.baz.extra_args?)
```

In the case of external calls with lots of arguments (looking at you, `fzf`) this would make a difference.

I could tackle that, provided the maintainers are okay with this new behaviour.

I would also 

### Describe the solution you'd like

In the following, `null`-valued expressions are just considered empty collections, and no longer trigger an error:

- `[1 2 3 ...(null)]`
- `^foo ...(null)`
- `wrapped-nu-command ...(null)`
- `{a: foo, b: bar, ...(null)}`

### Describe alternatives you've considered

Fully explicit `if` statements, or manually inserting the `default` of the correct type, with the aforementioned shortcomings.

### Additional context and details

_No response_
nushell/nushell#16283.title: Treating `null`-valued expressions in list and record spreads as empty collections

### Bug report form

### Describe the bug

in a table, when using `| sort-by foo` where `foo` is a float column, I am getting a rust panic:

```
Error:   × Main thread panicked.
  ├─▶ at library/core/src/slice/sort/shared/smallsort.rs:860:5
  ╰─▶ user-provided comparison function does not correctly implement a total order
  help: set the `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` environment variable to display a backtrace.
```

### How to reproduce

[issue.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22522815/issue.json)

if you do this to the attached file, the issue should occur:
`open issue.json | sort-by score`

The attached file has nothing special to it, at least as per my knowledge.


### Expected behavior

The table is sorted by the score column in an ascending order

nushell/nushell#17349.body:
### Basics

- [x] I have done a basic search through the issue tracker to find similar or related issues.
- [x] I have made myself familiar with the available features of Nushell for the particular area this enhancement request touches.

### Related problem

_No response_

### Describe the solution you'd like

I found that in Nushell, any command prepended with a leading space is not saved to `history.txt`. 

Compared to other shell, like bash, the behavior is controlled by `HISTCONTROL`:
- By default, Bash **records** commands with leading spaces.
- Users can explicitly set `HISTCONTROL=ignorespace` to hide them. 

### Describe alternatives you've considered

I think nushell should at least offer the same level of configurability.

### Additional context and details

_No response_
nushell/nushell#17349.title: Commands that start with a leading space are not saved to history.txt

### Bug report form

- [x] I have done a basic search of the issue tracker to find any existing issues that are similar.
- [x] I have checked that my version is at least the latest stable release available via my installation method.

### Describe the bug

[A comment from fncred] made me realize that `it` is actually not a reserved variable by default.  This can cause funky behavior.


[c]: https://github.com/nushell/nushell/issues/17372#issuecomment-3765679533

### How to reproduce

If `it` is used after getting assigned by `let` or `mut`, it'll get deleted

```nushell
~> let $it = 1
~> $it
1
~> ls | where $it == 1
╭────────────╮
│ empty list │
╰────────────╯
~> $it
Error: nu::shell::variable_not_found

  × Variable not found
   ╭─[entry #4:1:1]
 1 │ $it
   · ─┬─
   ·  ╰── variable not found
   ╰────
```

### Expected behavior

It probably makes sense to make `it` a reserved variable name too.

### Configuration

nushell/nushell#17554.body:
### Basics

- [x] I have done a basic search through the issue tracker to find similar or related issues.
- [x] I have made myself familiar with the available features of Nushell for the particular area this enhancement request touches.

### Related problem

Most of `http` commands (post, get, delete, patch) have the `--full` and `--allow-error`.

this 2 flags allow proper error handling.

Both `http head` and `http option` don't have this options making them a little bit useless.

### Describe the solution you'd like

I would like that the `--full` and `--allow-error` flag to be implemented for the `http head` and `http option` command.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

metadata of the request can be accessible inside the catch block of a try-catch block like the exit code inside the `pipefail` experimental option.

example:

```nushell
try { http head https://example.org/don-t/exist } catch {|e| print $e.http_response }
```

### Additional context and details

_No response_
nushell/nushell#17554.title: `http head` and `http options` missing --full and --allow-error

nushell/nushell#17780.body:
This is a tracking issue about how certain commands should interact with metadata. Feel free to comment if you have different opinions or if you can think of more commands that should interact with metadata

- [x] `<range> | first`/`last` shouldn't clear `content_type`
  - https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/17875
- [x] `<list/table/range> | first`/`last`/`get <index>` should keep all metadata.
  - https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/17875
- [x] `get name` should get rid of `datasource-ls` or `path-columns`, but not `get <index>`
  - https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/17866
- [x] `skip 0` should not clear content-type
  - https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/17867
- [x] `reject name` remove "name" from `path_columns`
  - https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/17867
- [x] `detect type` should keep `content_type` metadata if it cannot convert the value
  - https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/17906

The main idea is that we handle the metadata that needs handling, and always keep metadata if it doesn't make sense to be there (e.g. `content_type` on range)
nushell/nushell#17780.title: Tracking Issue: interaction with metadata

### Bug report form

- [x] I have done a basic search of the issue tracker to find any existing issues that are similar.
- [x] I have checked that my version is at least the latest stable release available via my installation method.

### Describe the bug

Hi, when parsing strings with `parse`, escaping the '{' character with `(char lbrace)` does not work. Escaping with `(char rbrace)`, `(char pipe)`, etc. does indeed work.

### How to reproduce

```
> "1234|56" | parse $'{a}(char pipe){b}'
╭───┬──────┬────╮
│ # │  a   │ b  │
├───┼──────┼────┤
│ 0 │ 1234 │ 56 │
╰───┴──────┴────╯
> "1234}56" | parse $'{a}(char rbrace){b}'
╭───┬──────┬────╮
│ # │  a   │ b  │
├───┼──────┼────┤
│ 0 │ 1234 │ 56 │
╰───┴──────┴────╯
> "1234{56" | parse $'{a}(char lbrace){b}'
╭────────────╮
│ empty list │
╰────────────╯
>

```

### Expected behavior

Escaping '{' should work like other characters.

### Configuration

nushell/nushell#17824.body:
### Bug report form

- [x] I have done a basic search of the issue tracker to find any existing issues that are similar.
- [x] I have checked that my version is at least the latest stable release available via my installation method.

### Describe the bug

I am not quite sure if this is a bug but it caused me headaches that I don't know how to resolve.
So, apparently, when passing arguments containing newlines to a nushell script that accepts `...args`, nushell interprets each line as its own command? I tried this with bash and bash seems fine with it even though I know nushell isn't really trying to do what bash does.

### How to reproduce

1. Write nushell script like so and make it executable:
  ```nushell
  #!/usr/bin/env nu
  
  def main [...args: string] {
    print ...($args)
  }
  ```
2. Run `./test.nu a b "c\nd"`
3. Error with output:
  ```text
  a
  b
  c
  Error: nu::shell::external_command
  
    × External command failed
     ╭─[<commandline>:2:1]
   1 │ main a b c
   2 │ d
     · ┬
     · ╰── Command `d` not found
     ╰────
    help: Did you mean `cd`?
  ```

### Expected behavior

I'd expect nushell to print normally and not error out.

With a script like so:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
echo "$@"
```

and invocation `./test.sh a b "c\nd"` bash happily prints out:

```text
a b c
d
```

. I know that technically when passing arguments like so to `print` it should print out probably something like

```text
a
b
c
d
```

and not `a b c` on the same line as bash because I am using splatting. That being said, I was not expecting it to error out.

### Configuration

| key                  | value                                                                                                       |
| -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| version              | 0.111.0                                                                                                     |
| major                | 0                                                                                                           |
| minor                | 111                                                                                                         |
| patch                | 0                                                                                                           |
| branch               |                                                                                                             |
| commit_hash          |                                                                                                             |
| build_os             | linux-x86_64                                                                                                |
| build_target         | x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                                    |
| rust_version         | rustc 1.93.0 (254b59607 2026-01-19) (built from a source tarball)                                           |
| cargo_version        | cargo 1.93.0 (083ac5135 2025-12-15)                                                                         |
| build_time           | 1980-01-01 00:00:00 +00:00                                                                                  |
| build_rust_channel   | release                                                                                                     |
| allocator            | standard                                                                                                    |
| features             | default, mcp, network, plugin, rustls-tls, sqlite, trash-support                                            |
| installed_plugins    |                                                                                                             |
| experimental_options | example=false, reorder-cell-paths=true, pipefail=true, enforce-runtime-annotations=false, native-clip=false |

nushell/nushell#17854.body:
### Bug report form

- [x] I have done a basic search of the issue tracker to find any existing issues that are similar.
- [x] I have checked that my version is at least the latest stable release available via my installation method.

### Describe the bug

Might be related to #17015, but the details differ enough that I think a separate issue is appropriate.

If you define an `extern` for completion then define an alias with the same name (to, for example, always pass a certain flag) then trying to use the alias will cause an error that "shouldn't happen".

### How to reproduce

```
$ extern firefox [
  --new-window
  --private-window
]
$ alias old-firefox = firefox
$ alias firefox = old-firefox --private-window
$ firefox
Error: nu::shell::nushell_failed_spanned

  × Nushell failed: known external name not found.
   ╭─[repl_entry #4:1:1]
 1 │ firefox
   · ───┬───
   ·    ╰── could not find name for this command
   ╰────
  help: This shouldn't happen. Please file an issue: https://github.com/nushell/nushell/issues
$ old-firefox
old-firefox
Error: nu::shell::nushell_failed_spanned

  × Nushell failed: known external name not found.
   ╭─[repl_entry #5:1:1]
 1 │ old-firefox
   · ─────┬─────
   ·      ╰── could not find name for this command
   ╰────
  help: This shouldn't happen. Please file an issue: https://github.com/nushell/nushell/issues
$ 
```

Various permutations of this also fail, such as:

```
$ extern firefox [
  --new-window
  --private-window
]
$ alias old-firefox = firefox
$ alias firefox = firefox --private-window
$ firefox
Error: nu::shell::nushell_failed_spanned

  × Nushell failed: known external name not found.
   ╭─[repl_entry #4:1:1]
 1 │ firefox
   · ───┬───
   ·    ╰── could not find name for this command
   ╰────
  help: This shouldn't happen. Please file an issue: https://github.com/nushell/nushell/issues
$ old-firefox
old-firefox
Error: nu::shell::nushell_failed_spanned

  × Nushell failed: known external name not found.
   ╭─[repl_entry #5:1:1]
 1 │ old-firefox
   · ─────┬─────
   ·      ╰── could not find name for this command
   ╰────
  help: This shouldn't happen. Please file an issue: https://github.com/nushell/nushell/issues
$ 
```

The aliasing itself is fine, this only happens when there is an `extern`:

```
$ alias firefox = firefox --private-window
$ firefox
$ 
```

Swapping the `alias` and the `extern` prevents the error, but the `extern` causes the `alias` to be forgotten:

```
$ alias old-firefox = firefox
$ alias firefox = old-firefox --private-window
$ firefox
# Opens private window :D
$ help aliases
╭────┬─────────────┬──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┬─────────┬─────────────────╮
│ # │    name     │          expansion           │               description                │ decl_id │ aliased_decl_id │
├────┼─────────────┼──────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┼─────────┼─────────────────┤
│ 0 │ firefox     │ old-firefox --private-window │ Alias for `old-firefox --private-window` │     758 │                 │
│ 1 │ old-firefox │ firefox                      │ Alias for `firefox`                      │     757 │                 │
╰────┴─────────────┴──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┴─────────┴─────────────────╯
$ extern firefox [
  --new-window
  --private-window
]
$ firefox
# Opens normal window :(
$ help aliases
╭───┬─────────────┬────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┬─────────┬─────────────────╮
│ # │    name     │       expansion        │            description             │ decl_id │ aliased_decl_id │
├───┼─────────────┼────────────────────────┼────────────────────────────────────┼─────────┼─────────────────┤
│ 0 │ old-firefox │ firefox                │ Alias for `firefox`                │     757 │                 │
╰───┴─────────────┴────────────────────────┴────────────────────────────────────┴─────────┴─────────────────╯
$ 
```

### Expected behavior

The alias should be able to be used without causing an error.

### Configuration

| key                  | value                                                                                                       |
| -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| version              | 0.111.0                                                                                                     |
| major                | 0                                                                                                           |
| minor                | 111                                                                                                         |
| patch                | 0                                                                                                           |
| branch               |                                                                                                             |
| commit_hash          |                                                                                                             |
| build_os             | linux-x86_64                                                                                                |
| build_target         | x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                                    |
| rust_version         | rustc 1.93.0 (254b59607 2026-01-19) (built from a source tarball)                                           |
| cargo_version        | cargo 1.93.0 (083ac5135 2025-12-15)                                                                         |
| build_time           | 1980-01-01 00:00:00 +00:00                                                                                  |
| build_rust_channel   | release                                                                                                     |
| allocator            | standard                                                                                                    |
| features             | default, mcp, network, plugin, rustls-tls, sqlite, trash-support                                            |
| installed_plugins    |                                                                                                             |
| experimental_options | example=false, reorder-cell-paths=true, pipefail=true, enforce-runtime-annotations=false, native-clip=false |
nushell/nushell#17854.title: Aliasing an extern causes an error when the alias is used ("known external name not found").

nushell/nushell#17865.body: ### Basics - [x] I have done a basic search through the issue tracker to find similar or related issues. - [x] I have made myself familiar with the available features of Nushell for the particular area this enhancement request touches. ### Related problem Most other colors that Nu uses in its interface are configurable, both inputs (e.g. syntax highlighting) and outputs (tables, search results, typed values). This is great for customized themes, for personal preference and accessibility. One exception is the pretty hex viewer that is used by default for `binary` output. It uses colors that are [hardcoded](https://github.com/nushell/nushell/blob/main/crates/nu-pretty-hex/src/pretty_hex.rs#L114-L125) and cannot be changed by the user. Additionally, there appears no way to disable them without piping the output through `ansi strip`: ### Describe the solution you'd like I propose three steps: 1. Respect `$env.config.use_ansi_coloring` (and related environment variables) in the pretty hex viewer, like the rest of Nushell 2. Add some new `$env.config.color_config` keys, which can be used to customize these colors. I'd propose one of these naming schemes ("flat", as recommended in https://github.com/nushell/nushell/issues/8703#issuecomment-2464916854): - My preference, I think (based on the title row): ```nushell $env.config.color_config.binary_null $env.config.color_config.binary_printable # or binary_ascii_printable $env.config.color_config.binary_whitespace # or binary_ascii_whitespace $env.config.color_config.binary_ascii_other $env.config.color_config.binary_non_ascii ``` - `$env.config.color_config.hex_*` - Something else? open to suggestions here > Related question: is `$env.config.color_config.binary` actually used for anything? From what I can tell, `shape_binary` is used for syntax highlighting input, but I can't seem to find anything that uses this color for output. 3. Update configs (default, dark + light theme) as needed to make the defaults introspectable / extensible. I might also suggest the light theme uses a different color for `non_ascii` (yellow can be hard to read on a white/off-white background), although perhaps that's more of an issue for the terminal emulator theme to figure out. ### Describe alternatives you've considered Leave the colors non-configurable but still support `$env.config.use_ansi_coloring = false` / `auto`. It seems like just about every other color Nushell produces is configurable (except maybe in error messages, although that would be nice too) so I think this just brings the hex viewer up to parity with everything else. ### Additional context and details I'd like to try contributing this change myself, if the plan sounds reasonable to maintainers. I've spent a little time looking through the related code, so I think I have a pretty good idea of what's needed to make the change, but I haven't contributed to Nushell directly yet so happy to take pointers!
nushell/nushell#17865.title: color_config parameters for pretty hex viewer (binary stream)

nushell/nushell#17916.body:
### Bug report form

- [x] I have done a basic search of the issue tracker to find any existing issues that are similar.
- [x] I have checked that my version is at least the latest stable release available via my installation method.

### Describe the bug

(since looking at this I've realized the scope of this issue is wider, but I'll describe the original problem.)

in the repl, an invalid part of commands is highlighted in red. however some errors are only highlighted for internal commands but not for external.

### How to reproduce

in the REPL, type:
1. `ps out>| $in`
2. `^ps out>| $in`

the first one has `out>|` etc. code marked in red, the second doesn't
either way, when sending the  command, the error is: `expected `|`.  Redirecting stdout to a pipe is the same as normal piping.`

### Expected behavior

I expected it to detect the error since
1. it detects it in the internal case
2. this code is always invalid, regardless of what external is being called

### Configuration

| key                  | value                                                                                                                                                                |
| -------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| version              | 0.111.1                                                                                                                                                              |
| major                | 0                                                                                                                                                                    |
| minor                | 111                                                                                                                                                                  |

| installed_plugins    | desktop_notifications 0.107.0, formats 0.107.0, highlight 1.4.9+0.107.0, json_path 0.17.1, polars 0.107.0, query 0.107.0, regex 0.19.0, skim 0.22.0, strutils 0.15.0 |
| experimental_options | example=false, reorder-cell-paths=true, pipefail=true, enforce-runtime-annotations=false, native-clip=false, cell-path-types=false                                   |
nushell/nushell#17916.title: No error highlighting when calling external program

nushell/nushell#17921.body:
`http get` (without `--raw` flag) should automatically parse responses based on their content-type. While this works with `application/nuon`, it does not with `application/x-nuon` which is the [correct mime type for nuon data format](https://www.nushell.sh/lang-guide/chapters/mime_types.html).

Discovered thanks to https://github.com/nushell/nushell/pull/17899#pullrequestreview-4017702187
nushell/nushell#17921.title: `http` commands do not handle responses with `application/x-nuon` content-type correctly

nushell/nushell#17942.body:
### Bug report form

- [x] I have done a basic search of the issue tracker to find any existing issues that are similar.
- [x] I have checked that my version is at least the latest stable release available via my installation method.

### Describe the bug

On version 0.111.0, invoking `nu` to run a script while also activating experimental options fails with an error about the experimental options.

This problem does not occur on v0.110.0.

### How to reproduce

```nu
nu -v
0.111.0

echo 'print "hello world"' o> test.nu

nu test.nu
hello world

nu --experimental-options '[example=true]' test.nu
Error: nu::shell::error

  × Invalid value for `--experimental-options`
  help: Valid experimental options: example, reorder-cell-paths, pipefail, enforce-runtime-annotations, native-clip
```

### Expected behavior

It should activate the experimental options *and* run the script. 

### Configuration

| key                  | value                                                                                                       |
| -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| version              | 0.111.0                                                                                                     |
| major                | 0                                                                                                           |
| minor                | 111                                                                                                         |
| patch                | 0                                                                                                           |
| branch               |                                                                                                             |
| commit_hash          | 6324e01116f46183dbeb478ed4e76523378b2052                                                                    |
| build_os             | linux-x86_64                                                                                                |
| build_target         | x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                                    |
| rust_version         | rustc 1.91.1 (ed61e7d7e 2025-11-07)                                                                         |
| rust_channel         | 1.91.1-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu                                                                             |
| cargo_version        | cargo 1.91.1 (ea2d97820 2025-10-10)                                                                         |
| build_time           | 2026-02-28 23:42:29 +00:00                                                                                  |
| build_rust_channel   | release                                                                                                     |
| allocator            | standard                                                                                                    |
| features             | default, mcp, network, plugin, rustls-tls, sqlite, static-link-openssl, trash-support                       |
| installed_plugins    |                                                                                                             |
| experimental_options | example=false, reorder-cell-paths=true, pipefail=true, enforce-runtime-annotations=false, native-clip=false |
nushell/nushell#17942.title: Cannot run script with experimental options in 0.111.0

nushell/nushell#17952.body:
> @cptpiepmatz Perhaps we could run `cargo check` in CI for these crates to catch issues like these?

Hmm, running `cargo check` in the root of the repo doesn't catch it for me. They are it seems by purpose in different workspaces. I'll look into updating CI regardless, we should catch that.

_Originally posted by @cptpiepmatz in https://github.com/nushell/nushell/issues/17950#issuecomment-4169246385_
nushell/nushell#17952.title: ci: run cargo clippy for fuzz crates

nushell/nushell#17953.body:
### Bug report form

- [x] I have done a basic search of the issue tracker to find any existing issues that are similar.
- [x] I have checked that my version is at least the latest stable release available via my installation method.

### Describe the bug

```
ls | insert foo { error make { msg: 'boo' } } | reject -o kek
Error: nu::shell::column_not_found

  × Cannot find column 'kek'
   ╭─[repl_entry #17:1:6]
 1 │ ls | insert foo { error make { msg: 'boo' } } | reject -o kek
   ·      ───┬──                                               ─┬─
   ·         │                                                  ╰── cannot find column 'kek'
   ·         ╰── value originates here
   ╰────
```

### How to reproduce

see above

### Expected behavior

`reject -o` by definition must not care about a missing column

### Configuration

| minor                | 111                                                                                                         |
